hypothesis: Selectively corrupting or masking a single modality during X-MoPA's cross-modal prediction pretraining—rather than always providing two complete modalities to predict the third—will force the model to learn more robust, modality-agnostic latent representations that exhibit superior generalization to materials property prediction tasks where only a single modality (e.g., only a crystal graph without XRD or text) is available at inference time, measurably outperforming the standard X-MoPA fine-tuned under the same single-modality constraint.
